evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 4 conditions in correct order (Control→low→high→Combo), violin+box overlay, white-line medians, jittered points at alpha=0.35, and Mann-Whitney significance brackets included.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set anywhere in the script; matplotlib defaults to Type 3 (non-selectable) fonts; font family falls back to DejaVu Sans default.
evidence.palette: Blue/orange pair is colorblind-safe, but #55A868 (green) and #C44E52 (red) are used for categorical conditions Drug-A 50uM and Combo — problematic for red-green colorblind viewers (~8% of males).
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,5) with tight_layout() is compact; three stacked significance brackets push the top margin noticeably but no overlapping labels; bottom anchored at 0 is appropriate.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes all visual elements and states n=100; significance notation is explained; however, caption never states the key finding (e.g., which group differs most or by how much).
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is 28 KB (consistent with real vector content), but pdf.fonttype is not set to 42, so text is embedded as Type 3 bitmap glyphs — not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: All four required outputs present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (28 KB), figure.png (186 KB), caption.tex (397 B), gen_figure.py (2.9 KB); bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains violin, box, jitter, and significance conventions and states n=100 per group, but provides no specific numerical finding (e.g., median values or fold-change for Combo vs Control).

overall_strengths: Complete implementation of the brief: correct condition order, all three visual layers (violin, box, jitter), and optional significance brackets all present.
All required output files exist and are non-trivial; tight_layout and spine removal give a clean, publication-style appearance.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype) means text in both PDF and SVG outputs is non-selectable Type 3 — a hard requirement for most journals and vector editing workflows.
Green (#55A868) / red (#C44E52) color pair for categorical conditions is not safe for red-green colorblind readers; caption also omits a specific quantitative finding.
